_comment: Every behavior/topic and named term ever used. The autonomous generator reads this, invents only NEW topics, and appends here. Prevents duplication forever.

used.topic: checking your balance only after you buy, at 1am, to brace not plan
knowing your balance as a vibe, not a number
one slip on day 8 makes you declare the month ruined and stop
the folder where unread bank emails pile up as dread
open the app, look at one number, close it — look don't fix
a savings account named 'Savings' is easy to raid; rename it with a story
muting your bank's alerts and calling it self-care; turn deposits back on
auto-saving Monday then pulling it back by Friday; move transfer to payday
deleting the saved card from the one site that gets you at midnight
a 23-category budget is an unpaid job; cut to three numbers
mid-month bill ambush; move one due date to after payday
cancelling the subscription you keep for the person you meant to become
Leaving one card 'for emergencies' that you have quietly been living on since March
Waiting until the card gets declined to admit the money is gone
Paying the minimum and calling it 'handled' for the month
Screenshotting a bill instead of paying it, then never opening the screenshot
Choosing the self-checkout so no cashier watches your total climb
Letting a friend cover it now and both of you agreeing to 'figure it out later'
Timing the purchase for right after a deposit so it feels free
Reading only the 'amount due' line and skipping the part that says why
You build the whole budget for a paycheck that only shows up in good months
You wait for the 'clean start' month, so nothing starts
You keep the plan only in your head, so it changes shape to fit the purchase
You track spending so carefully that tracking becomes the whole plan
You leave a buffer, then treat the buffer as spendable
You save whatever is left at the end, and nothing is ever left
You aim the plan at a number so big it feels fake, so you never start
You quietly move the goalpost every time you get close to hitting it
screenshot the one bill that scares you most
add up your three smallest subscriptions out loud
turn on the low-balance alert you keep declining
open the folder of receipts you never open
unhide the app you moved to the last home screen
write the actual due date on the bill, not 'soon'
read one line of the terms on your own subscription
star the one recurring charge you forgot you agreed to
